speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Shashi Tharoor delivered a passionate speech in Parliament arguing that democracy is not merely a system of governance but a way of life.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: The idea of India encompasses diversity tolerance and the constitutional guarantee of rights for every citizen said Tharoor in Parliament.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Tharoor argued that India pluralist democratic traditions reflect centuries of philosophical thought about coexistence and tolerance.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Education must be decolonised and Britain owes India a historical reparation for centuries of exploitation said Shashi Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Tharoor presented evidence of the drain of wealth from India to Britain during colonial rule amounting to trillions of dollars.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Free speech and a free press are the oxygen of democracy said Tharoor warning against attempts to muzzle dissent.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Any attempt to curtail journalistic freedom or silence political opposition is an attack on the republic itself said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Gender equality is not an option but a prerequisite for national development said Shashi Tharoor in his parliamentary address.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Women political representation must increase substantially in Parliament and state legislatures said Tharoor advocating for reservation.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: India soft power including cinema yoga cuisine and literature is its greatest diplomatic asset said Tharoor at a cultural summit.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Bollywood the yoga movement and Indian cuisine have done more for global perception of India than any diplomatic mission said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Social justice requires affirmative action to correct historical wrongs of caste discrimination said Tharoor in his address.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Caste discrimination persists in modern India and must be confronted honestly without political convenience said Shashi Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Healthcare is a right not a privilege and universal health coverage must be the cornerstone of India social policy said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: India healthcare system needs massive investment in primary care rural health and preventive medicine said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Climate justice means wealthy nations that caused historical emissions must finance adaptation in the Global South said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: India must lead the demand for climate finance from developed countries at international negotiations said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: The digital divide threatens to leave millions of Indians behind and bridging connectivity gaps is as important as roads said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Tharoor spoke about the need for digital literacy programs to ensure technology benefits reach rural and marginalised communities.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Parliament must debate vigorously and hold the government accountable on all matters of national importance said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Tharoor argued that the quality of parliamentary debate has declined and legislators must engage more substantively with policy.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: India secularism is a fundamental feature of the Constitution and must be protected against communal polarisation said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: The diversity of India is its strength not a weakness and no single cultural identity can define our pluralist nation said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Tharoor delivered a lecture on Indian history challenging the colonial narrative and celebrating indigenous intellectual traditions.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: India contribution to world civilisation in mathematics astronomy philosophy and literature is immense said Shashi Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: The rights of minorities linguistic religious and cultural must be protected under the constitutional framework said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Tharoor argued for stronger federal structures and more financial autonomy for state governments in India.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Electoral reforms including state funding of elections and stricter campaign finance laws are urgently needed said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: India foreign policy must be guided by values of democracy human rights and rule of law not just transactional interests said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Tharoor spoke about the role of United Nations in maintaining peace and the need for India to actively shape its agenda.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: The treatment of Indias colonial history in textbooks and public discourse needs to be honest and comprehensive said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Tharoor argued that the partition of India was a direct consequence of British divide and rule policies over decades.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: India environmental laws must be strengthened to protect forests rivers and biodiversity from commercial exploitation said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Tharoor raised concerns about surveillance technologies and the right to privacy as a fundamental constitutional right.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Judicial independence and the separation of powers must be maintained as pillars of Indian democracy said Shashi Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: India anti-corruption measures must go beyond rhetoric to structural reforms that reduce discretionary power said Tharoor.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: Tharoor spoke in favour of decriminalising personal choices that harm no others as a matter of individual liberty.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: The rights of LGBTQ citizens must be fully recognised under Indian law said Tharoor supporting equality before the law.

speaker: Tharoor
full_text: India needs a national urban policy to manage the explosive growth of cities and ensure livable urban environments said Tharoor.

full_text: Tharoor delivered the keynote at a literary festival celebrating Indian writing in English as a vital creative tradition.
